name: 1xlasm
description:
Two-entity giantess captioning skin. The xlgts woman is the larger figure; the xlasm man is the smaller adult-man partner. Trained for the gts dataset; carries a fine-tuned LoRA adapter for caption-quality on this domain.

JSON/MD split: this JSON is the machine-readable surface — entity tokens & phrases, label-group taxonomy, compose substitution tables, mechanical validators (forbidden vocab, body_type_words), concept matchers, model refs. Theme briefing — scene archetypes, captioner quirks, anti-pattern principles, stylistic intuition, exemplars — lives in the sibling file `conf/skins/1xlasm.md` and is the primary input at /img_caption Stage 1 (per-image prompt composition with Claude in the loop). New insights about the theme accumulate in the MD, not as new JSON `rules` rows; the JSON should drift toward a stable structural surface over time.

primary.description: The giantess woman, the larger figure. Trigger phrase: '{entities.primary.phrase}'.
rules: Identify her ONLY as '{entities.primary.phrase}'. Do not use 'tall', 'large', 'giant', 'huge', 'towering', 'enormous', 'massive', 'colossal', or any numerical heights — the trigger phrase carries her size concept.

Do not use these words unless an explicit instruction later in this prompt authorizes them: 'muscular', 'bodybuilder', 'busty', 'voluptuous', 'slim', 'slender', 'lean', 'curvy', 'hourglass', 'cleavage', 'large breasts', 'big breasts'. Default to omitting body-type and breast descriptions entirely.

attribute.description: Body-type attributes of the woman. Opt-in: only describe in the caption when the corresponding label is set on the image.

interaction.description: The interaction between the {entities.primary.phrase} and the {entities.secondary.phrase} is the central subject of every image.
rules: Open every caption with a single COMPLETE sentence (subject + verb + object) that explicitly names BOTH '{entities.primary.phrase}' AND '{entities.secondary.phrase}' together — e.g. 'This image features a {entities.primary.phrase} and a {entities.secondary.phrase}.', 'The {entities.primary.phrase} holds the {entities.secondary.phrase} in her palm.'. If unsure how to combine the two, default to the literal opener 'This image features a {entities.primary.phrase} and a {entities.secondary.phrase}.' as the first sentence. Never a noun-phrase fragment. Setting, pose, clothing, hair, breasts, background, lighting, and camera angle come strictly AFTER this opener.

Each figure's clothing state is independent and must be evaluated from the image directly. A figure is "naked" ONLY when no clothing at all is visible on them. If a figure is wearing any of: dress, skirt, top, shirt, tank top, blouse, lingerie, bra, panties, thong, stockings, tights, hosiery, gloves, robe, gown, harness, jacket, coat, leotard, corset, swimsuit — that figure is NOT naked, even if much skin is exposed. Apply "naked" sparingly, only to a fully unclothed figure, ONCE at the figure's first reference (e.g. "The naked {entities.secondary.phrase} …"). Never repeat "naked", "nude", "undressed", or "unclothed" for that same figure afterward. Anti-pattern: image shows the {entities.primary.phrase} wearing a dress or lingerie → "The {entities.primary.phrase}, naked, …" is WRONG; she is clothed. Anti-pattern (repetition): "The {entities.primary.phrase} holds the naked {entities.secondary.phrase}. The naked {entities.secondary.phrase} smiles." is WRONG — the second sentence must say "The {entities.secondary.phrase} smiles."
Describe the interaction in detail using specific verbs.
Use compositional position references ('at her ankle', 'at her hip level', 'in her palm', 'on her shoulder') — composition, not size.
Do not invent details that are not visible.

Preserve every verb and body-part reference the user hint names verbatim — do NOT collapse 'thumb and index finger' to 'hand', 'palm' to 'hand', 'arm' to 'hand', 'wrapping around' to 'between', or 'inserted' to 'positioned'. Use the same body-part level (arm vs hand, palm vs hand) the hint gives.

Hint sentinel: a SceneImage whose `hints` field is the literal string 'none' (case-insensitive) means no hint applies — the runtime drops the user-hint preamble entirely for that image. Treat it as if no hint were provided.

Never emit the construction 'is naked, with ...' (e.g. 'The {entities.secondary.phrase} is naked, with an erect penis'). When indicating nudity, place 'naked' inside the noun phrase at first mention ('a naked {entities.secondary.phrase}', 'a naked {entities.primary.phrase}'). For attributes or body parts, use 'has' ('The {entities.secondary.phrase} has an erect penis'). Never emit the comma-bracketed appositive 'X, naked, Y' either; bind nudity to the noun phrase or drop it.
Never describe watermarks, logos, studio names, brand names, captions, subtitles, or any overlaid text present in the image. Ignore them as if they were not there.

When introducing the entities for the first time, prefer the opener 'This image features a {entities.primary.phrase} and a [naked] {entities.secondary.phrase}.' (or '... and a [naked] {entities.primary.phrase}.' when she is the nude one) — bundle the nudity adjective with the noun rather than asserting it later as a standalone sentence. Use 'naked' here only if the figure is fully unclothed.

When multiple pose labels apply to the same figure (e.g. 'lying' + 'on_back', or 'sitting' + 'legs_spread'), combine them into one fluent compound sentence ('lying on her back with her legs spread wide apart') rather than emitting each pose as its own standalone sentence. Avoid 'is positioned' as a filler verb; use a concrete spatial verb or omit.

proximity.description: The man is held in close proximity to a specific body part of the woman, without making contact or being inserted. Use these instead of touch.* when the geometry is 'near but not touching' — typically she holds him hovering near the named body part.

default_prompt: Write a detailed description of this image.
user_hint_preamble: In this image, the central interaction is: {hint} Describe this interaction first and in full detail, reflecting every verb and spatial relationship it names (do not collapse "wrapping around" into "between", do not collapse "inserted" into "positioned", do not collapse "squeeze" into "cup"). Use the same body-part references it gives you (arm vs hand, palm vs hand). Do not quote, restate, or comment on this note - just write the caption.

body_type_words._doc: Words that may only appear in a caption when the named label is on the image. Used by the validator to flag unauthorized body-type descriptions.

insertion.description: Any insertion-themed scene (i_* labels). Sub-concepts split by orifice.

general.description: Catch-all for labeled images that don't match any other concept.

_built.directive:
This image features two figures together: a xlgts woman and a xlasm man.

The giantess woman, the larger figure. Trigger phrase: 'xlgts woman'. Identify her ONLY as 'xlgts woman'. Do not use 'tall', 'large', 'giant', 'huge', 'towering', 'enormous', 'massive', 'colossal', or any numerical heights — the trigger phrase carries her size concept. Do not use these words unless an explicit instruction later in this prompt authorizes them: 'muscular', 'bodybuilder', 'busty', 'voluptuous', 'slim', 'slender', 'lean', 'curvy', 'hourglass', 'cleavage', 'large breasts', 'big breasts'. Default to omitting body-type and breast descriptions entirely.

The adult man, the smaller figure. Trigger phrase: 'xlasm man'. Always adult-proportioned regardless of apparent size. Identify him ONLY as 'xlasm man'. (or '... and a [naked] xlgts woman.' when she is the nude one) — bundle the nudity adjective with the noun rather than asserting it later as a standalone sentence.
